files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index ad3abf0..5a595ee 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -205,2 +205,9 @@ Day 196 / Week 28 observations:
 - Cancellations stayed high at 1,123, mostly S2 A, but larger quotas held cancellations roughly flat despite base growth.
 - Week 28 adjustment: start tier-8 R&D, keep ads/prices/capacity stable, significantly increase support, modestly increase dev, and post a legal/citation critical-SLA commitment.
+
+Day 203 / Week 29 observations:
+- Cash dropped to $684k after tier-8 R&D. Operating cash flow was only modestly positive because support/dev costs are now very high.
+- S2 content is still profitable, but cancellations spiked to 2,597, mostly S2 A (2,196). S2 issue age rose to 2.51 days, so the support queue is now hurting retention.
+- Peak usage was 13.2M/day, still below tier-5 capacity. Capacity is not the bottleneck.
+- Social response to citation SLA was mixed: some trust gains, but repeated requests for documented enforcement, retroactive priority, and credits.
+- Week 29 adjustment: pause new R&D, reduce paid S2 acquisition temporarily, increase S2 support coverage, keep dev high, and post that existing critical tickets get retroactive priority and credits.
